# Art As A Consequence (AAAC)

## What is AAAC?
Art As A Consequence (AAAC) is a ceramics studio and gallery by Dimitria Barrows, based in Zug, Switzerland (Crypto Valley). The studio produces handmade stoneware ceramics — functional sculptural vessels — that explore value formation in internet memes, crypto culture, economics, and onchain dynamics.

Each piece is unique, boldly glazed, and created with utility in mind. All artworks are NFC-chipped and onchain-registered on the Base blockchain, existing as physical-digital twins.

## Who is Dimitria Barrows?
Dimitria Barrows is a contemporary ceramic artist based in Zug, Switzerland. She graduated from ZHdK (Zürcher Hochschule der Künste) with a BA in Fine Arts in 2015 and attended workshops at the Royal Academy of Arts, London (2008–2010).

Barrows spent a decade working in the art industry — at Gagosian (London) and Galerie Thaddaeus Ropac (Paris/Salzburg) — handling exhibitions, sales, and artists' studios. She also ran an art advisory practice (Art As A Consequence, the former iteration) specializing in marketing, studio management, Web2/Web3 digitalization, and strategic partnerships.

Notable advisory clients included:
- The Oskar Schlemmer Theatre Archives
- Wolfgang Beltracchi

In 2024, she returned to her own creative practice, making ceramics under the AAAC name.

Twitter/X: @dimitria1337

## The Ceramics
- Medium: Handmade stoneware
- Each piece: unique, functional, sculptural
- NFC-chipped for physical-digital verification
- Registered on Base blockchain
- Themes: crypto culture, internet memes, value theory, onchain dynamics
- Available for purchase with international shipping

## Key Pages
- / — Homepage / gallery grid
- /work — Full collection view
- /about — Dimitria Barrows biography
- /piece/[slug] — Individual artwork detail page
- /blast-from-the-past — Archive of the former art advisory practice
- /blast-from-the-past/about — Art professional biography
- /blast-from-the-past/art-direction — Jeff Koons campaigns and strategic partnerships
- /blast-from-the-past/interviews — Conversations with artists, collectors, curators

## Key Themes
- Ceramics as collectible timestamps of crypto culture
- Physical-digital twins via NFC + blockchain registration
- Value formation in internet memes and financial systems
- Bridge between contemporary art world and web3 space
- Handcraft as counter-signal to purely digital assets

## Domain
https://artasaconsequence.net
